tool_args.label: Draft consolidated financial statements - MTA Interim Financial Statements

payload.title: Draft consolidated financial statements
payload.description: Independent Auditor's Review Report - Interim Financial Statements as of and for the Nine-Month Period Ended September 30, 2025

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metropolitan Transportation Authority
Independent Auditor's Review Report
Interim Financial Statements as of and for the Nine-Month Period Ended September 30, 2025

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Established under the New York Public Authorities Law, a public benefit corporation and a component unit of the State of New York whose mission is to continue, develop, and improve public transportation and to develop and implement a unified public transportation policy in the New York metropolitan area.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority ("MTA" or "MTA Group") was established under the New York Public Authorities Law and is a public benefit corporation and a component unit of the State of New York

payload.description: Provides subway and public bus service within the five boroughs of New York City, with its subsidiary Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority (MaBSTOA).

evidence_refs.source_quote: New York City Transit Authority ("MTA New York City Transit") and its subsidiary, Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority ("MaBSTOA"), provide subway and public bus service within the five boroughs of New York City.

payload.entity_name: The Long Island Rail Road Company
payload.description: Provides passenger transportation between New York City and Long Island.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Long Island Rail Road Company ("MTA Long Island Rail Road") provides passenger transportation between New York City ("NYC") and Long Island.

payload.description: Provides passenger transportation between NYC and suburban communities in Westchester, Dutchess, Putnam, Orange, and Rockland counties in NYS and New Haven and Fairfield counties in Connecticut.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North Commuter Railroad Company ("MTA Metro-North Railroad") provides passenger transportation between NYC and the suburban communities in Westchester, Dutchess, Putnam, Orange, and Rockland counties in New York State ("NYS") and New Haven and Fairfield counties in Connecticut.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer

payload.description: Operates seven toll bridges, two tunnels, and the Battery Parking Garage, all within the five boroughs of New York City.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority ("MTA Bridges and Tunnels" or "TBTA") operates seven toll bridges, two tunnels, and the Battery Parking Garage, all within the five boroughs of New York City.

payload.description: Operates certain bus routes in areas previously served by private bus operators pursuant to franchises granted by the City of New York.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Bus Company ("MTA Bus") operates certain bus routes in areas previously served by private bus operators pursuant to franchises granted by the City of New York.

payload.description: Provides passenger transportation between NYC and suburban communities in Westchester, Dutchess, Putnam, Orange, and Rockland counties in NYS and New Haven and Fairfield counties in Connecticut.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metro-North Commuter Railroad Company ("MTA Metro-North Railroad") provides passenger transportation between NYC and the suburban communities

payload.event_name: Central Business District Tolling Program commencement
payload.description: MTA Bridges and Tunnels began collecting CBDTP tolls for vehicles entering the Central Business District (south of 60th Street in Manhattan).

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Bridges and Tunnels began collecting CBDTP tolls on January 5, 2025

payload.claim_text: The Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP) was established by NYS legislation in 2019 to manage traffic congestion in Manhattan and achieve a minimum $15 billion of funding for projects identified in MTA's 2020-2024 Capital Program.
payload.subject: Central Business District Tolling Program
payload.description: CBDTP funding purpose and minimum funding requirement for 2020-2024 Capital Program

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Central Business District Tolling Program ("CBDTP") was established by New York State legislation in 2019 to both manage traffic congestion in Manhattan and be implemented in a manner that achieves a minimum $15 billion of funding for the projects identified in MTA's 2020-2024 Capital Program

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
